description_long: This house features a steeply pitched, double-gabled roof with reddish-brown clay tiles and two prominent dormer windows on each long side. The exterior walls are clad in a half-timbered style, with visible wooden beams and light-colored infill panels, evoking a traditional European or Tudor appearance. The structure is rectangular, with symmetrical window placement and a compact, sturdy form. The roof overhangs the walls, providing additional weather protection and architectural interest. Windows are multi-paned and evenly distributed, while the main entryway is subtly integrated into the facade. The overall design suggests a cozy, historic residential dwelling suitable for a village or old-town urban setting.
description: A traditional half-timbered house with a steep, tiled gable roof and symmetrical dormer windows, evoking a classic European style.
description_view_0: End view showing the steep gable roof, half-timbered facade, and two rows of windows, with the main entryway visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, tiled roof with two dormer windows and a row of evenly spaced windows below.
description_view_2: Opposite end view, similar to view 0, showing the gable roof and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, with the same dormer and window arrangement.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple wooden door integrated into the facade, no porch or steps, minimal decorative elements
